title: Ensure that the --authorization-mode argument is not set to AlwaysAllow

input_row.title: Ensure that the --authorization-mode argument is not set to AlwaysAllow
input_row.description: Do not allow all requests. Enable explicit authorization.
input_row.rationale: Kubelets, by default, allow all authenticated requests (even anonymous ones) without needing explicit authorization checks from the apiserver. You should restrict this behavior and only allow explicitly authorized requests.
input_row.audit: In OpenShift 4, the kublet config file is managed by the Machine Config Operator. By default, Unauthenticated/Unauthorized users have no access to OpenShift nodes. Run the following command: #In one terminal, run: oc proxy #Then in another terminal, run: for name in $(oc get nodes -ojsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}') do curl -sS http://127.0.0.1:8080/api/v1/nodes/$name/proxy/configz | jq - r '.kubeletconfig.authorization.mode' done # Alternative without oc proxy POD=$(oc -n openshift-kube-apiserver get pod -l app=openshift-kube-apiserver -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}') TOKEN=$(oc whoami -t) for name in $(oc get nodes -ojsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}') do oc exec -n openshift-kube-apiserver $POD -- curl -sS https://172.25.0.1/api/v1/nodes/$name/proxy/configz -k -H "Authorization: 215 | P a g e Bearer $TOKEN" | jq -r '.kubeletconfig.authorization.mode' done Verify that access is not successful.
input_row.remediation: None required. Unauthenticated/Unauthorized users have no access to OpenShift nodes.
input_row.default_value: By default, --authorization-mode argument is set to Webhook .

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The control can be verified automatically. The audit field provides a clear method to retrieve the required information about the `--authorization-mode` argument. The audit mentions commands (`oc proxy` and `curl`) that interact with OpenShift's API, which can be queried via the IBM Cloud CLI.

**PROGRAM NAME:** ibm_openshift_kubelet_authorization_mode_not_always_allow

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
1. Use the `oc` CLI command to run the necessary queries, as outlined in the audit section:
   - For the first method (using `oc proxy`):
     - Run: `oc proxy` in one terminal window to start the API proxy.
     - Then, in another terminal, execute:
       ```
       for name in $(oc get nodes -ojsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}'); do 
           curl -sS http://127.0.0.1:8080/api/v1/nodes/$name/proxy/configz | jq -r '.kubeletconfig.authorization.mode';
       done
       ```
   - For the second method (without `oc proxy`):
     - Get the pod name of `openshift-kube-apiserver`:
       ```
       POD=$(oc -n openshift-kube-apiserver get pod -l app=openshift-kube-apiserver -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}');
       ```
     - Retrieve the token for authentication:
       ```
       TOKEN=$(oc whoami -t);
       ```
     - Then, execute the following loop:
       ```
       for name in $(oc get nodes -ojsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}'); do 
           oc exec -n openshift-kube-apiserver $POD -- curl -sS https://172.25.0.1/api/v1/nodes/$name/proxy/configz -k -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" | jq -r '.kubeletconfig.authorization.mode';
       done
       ```
2. **Specific Resources to Enumerate:**
   - The `nodes` in the OpenShift cluster.

3. **Exact Configuration Properties/Settings to Validate:**
   - Check that the `authorization.mode` is not set to `AlwaysAllow`.

4. **Pass/Fail Criteria:**
   - The expected value for the `authorization.mode` property should NOT be `AlwaysAllow`. Any instance returning this indicates a compliance failure.

By following this automated audit approach, the compliance of the OpenShift Container Platform with respect to the authorization mode can be effectively verified.

**References for Implementation:**
- Official OpenShift documentation related to Machine Config Operator and Kubelet Config: 
  1. [Understanding Machine Config Operator](https://docs.openshift.com/container-platform/4.5/architecture/control-plane.html#understanding-machine-config-operator_control-plane)
  2. [Recommended host practices](https://docs.openshift.com/container-platform/4.5/scalability_and_performance/recommended-host-practices.html#create-a-kubeletconfig-crd-to-edit-kubelet-parameters_)
   
This automated approach leverages existing commands and the IBM Cloud CLI capabilities to ensure effective compliance auditing.
